background_concepts.why_needed: Most land-cover mapping papers treat land classification as a pixel-level segmentation problem using encoder-decoder CNNs (U-Net, DeepLab), but assume the reader already knows what semantic segmentation is and why per-pixel labeling differs from image classification.

suggested_resources.title: A Beginner's guide to Deep Learning based Semantic Segmentation using Keras
Get Started with Semantic Segmentation Using Deep Learning (MathWorks)

domain_name: Remote Sensing for Deforestation & Forest Change Detection

background_concepts.why_needed: These works assume familiarity with comparing co-registered image time series to flag pixels that changed, and increasingly build on foundation models like the Segment Anything Model (SAM), neither of which is explained from first principles.

suggested_resources.title: Self-Supervised Learning and Foundation Models (INRIA lecture slides, covers SAM)

domain_name: Forest Carbon & Biomass Estimation via Remote Sensing

background_concepts.why_needed: Biomass and carbon-stock papers repeatedly reference AGB density, canopy vertical structure, and spaceborne LiDAR (e.g. GEDI) as the ground-truth measurement backbone without explaining how these are measured or converted to carbon.

suggested_resources.title: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) — NASA Earthdata Basics
NASA ARSET: An Introduction to SAR and Its Applications

background_concepts.why_needed: Integrity critiques assume the reader understands the REDD+ 'payment-for-performance' offset mechanism, baselines, and the Monitoring/Reporting/Verification (MRV) process against which over-crediting is measured.

suggested_resources.title: What is REDD+ and why is it important? (ICVCM)
REDD+ FAQ: Explaining forestry climate projects (Carbon Market Watch)

domain_name: Community-Based Forest Governance, Tenure & Participatory Mapping
Community Climate Vulnerability & Adaptation Assessment Tools
Public Works & Social Protection as Carbon Co-Benefits

background_concepts.why_needed: Papers linking social protection to carbon co-benefits assume knowledge of India's MGNREGA — its demand-driven 100-day wage guarantee, gram panchayat implementation, and its natural-resource-management works — as the institutional vehicle for sequestration.

suggested_resources.title: MGNREGS — Drishti IAS Government Schemes explainer
MGNREGS — Rural Development Department scheme brief

domain_name: Deforestation Detection & Driver Classification from Satellite Imagery

background_concepts.concept: Vision Transformers vs. CNNs for image classification
background_concepts.why_needed: Driver-classification papers (e.g. ForestViT, rotation-equivariant models) assume the reader knows the architectural distinction between convolutional networks and transformer-based image classifiers, and why equivariance/attention matter for overhead imagery.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation (covers CNN backbones)

domain_name: Forest Carbon Stock Estimation & Carbon Credit Integrity

background_concepts.why_needed: Deforestation-baseline and carbon-credit papers rely on estimating a counterfactual (what would have happened without protection), often via synthetic control or state-space causal methods, which are used but not taught.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Synthetic Control Methods (Tilburg Science Hub)
Synthetic control — Causal Inference Course notes

background_concepts.concept: Diameter at breast height (DBH) and forest inventory metrics
background_concepts.why_needed: Tree-measurement papers estimate structural attributes like DBH and canopy height as inputs to allometric biomass equations, assuming the reader knows these standard forest-mensuration metrics and how depth/LiDAR data map to them.

suggested_resources.title: Airborne Lidar Estimation of Aboveground Biomass (explains DBH-based allometric equations)

domain_name: Foundation Models & Data-Efficient Learning for Earth Observation

background_concepts.why_needed: These CS-methods papers build on visual prompt tuning, few-shot/weak supervision, and pretrained encoders, all of which assume the reader understands self-supervised representation learning and the pretrain-then-adapt foundation-model paradigm.

suggested_resources.title: Self-Supervised Learning and Foundation Models (INRIA lecture slides)

background_concepts.why_needed: The drought-resilience forecasting work interprets model drivers using Shapley values, which are borrowed from cooperative game theory and assumed known rather than explained.

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to SHAP Values and ML Interpretability (DataCamp)
SHAP — Interpretable Machine Learning book chapter

domain_name: Community Co-Design & Indigenous Knowledge in Climate Monitoring
Interpretability & Verification for Climate Finance and Conservation

background_concepts.concept: Metric learning and weak supervision for verification
background_concepts.why_needed: Verification systems (TrueBranch, Xingu, GainForest) rely on metric-learning embeddings and weakly-supervised labels to audit conservation claims, assuming the reader knows these representation-learning and label-efficient techniques.

suggested_resources.title: A Primer on Self-supervised Learning (covers representation/metric learning)
